bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves that become angular, yellow halos around spots, spots may merge
treatment: Remove infected leaves and plants immediately
Water at soil level (avoid overhead irrigation)
Rotate crops with non-solanaceous plants for 2-3 years
bacterial_spot.prevention: Use resistant varieties, proper spacing, avoid working with wet plants

early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings (target-like), yellowing leaves, starts on lower leaves
treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicides every 7-10 days
Apply mulch to prevent soil splash
Improve air circulation through proper spacing
Apply neem oil as organic alternative
early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops annually, remove plant debris, avoid overhead watering

late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked gray-green spots, white fungal growth on underside in humidity, rapid plant collapse
treatment: Destroy infected plants immediately (do not compost)
Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb
Remove volunteer tomato and potato plants nearby
Water early morning so leaves dry quickly
late_blight.prevention: Use certified disease-free transplants, monitor weather for blight forecasts

healthy.symptoms: Green, vibrant leaves, no spots or discoloration, normal growth pattern
care_tips: Continue regular watering (1-2 inches per week)
Prune lower leaves for better air circulation
fertilizers: Calcium nitrate to prevent blossom end rot
